title: main.c LED error pattern undocumented for field debugging
description:
main.c:129-137 blinks LED 3 times on USB failure:
```c
for (int i = 0; i < 3; i++) {
    gpio_pin_set_dt(&led, 1);
    k_sleep(K_MSEC(100));
    ...
}
```

What goes wrong: A field technician seeing the blink pattern has no way to know what it means without source code access. If additional error patterns are added later, distinguishing them becomes guesswork.

Suggested: Add a comment block at the top of main.c documenting LED patterns:
```c
/*
 * LED diagnostic patterns:
 * - 3 rapid blinks (100ms each): USB CDC initialization failed
 * - Solid on during init: normal startup
 * - Off after init: ready
 */
```

This creates a single reference point for all patterns.
